
We can abuse GPO misconfigurations to achieve the following:
- Adding additional rights to a user (e.g. SeImpersonatePrivilege)
- Adding a local admin user
- Creating an immediate scheduled task to perform any action

Enumeration
---
PowerView to get a list of GPOs ([https://github.com/PowerShellMafia/PowerSploit/tree/master/Recon](https://github.com/PowerShellMafia/PowerSploit/tree/master/Recon))
```
Get-DomainGPO |select displayname
```
PowerShell
```
Get-GPO -All | Select DisplayName
```

Finding writable GPOs
```
$sid=Convert-NameToSid "Domain Users"
```
```
Get-DomainGPO | Get-ObjectAcl | ?{$_.SecurityIdentifier -eq $sid}
```
Convert GUID to Name
```
Get-GPO -Guid 7CA9C789-14CE-46E3-A722-83F4097AF532
```
![](data:image/png;base64,)

Abuse
---
We can now use BloodHound to find the GPO and see how we can abuse it, we can use the tool SharpGPOAbuse to do so:
[https://github.com/ReversecLabs/SharpGPOAbuse](https://github.com/ReversecLabs/SharpGPOAbuse)

Adding new local admin:
[https://medium.com/@raphaeltzy13/group-policy-object-gpo-abuse-windows-active-directory-privilege-escalation-51d8519a13d7](https://medium.com/@raphaeltzy13/group-policy-object-gpo-abuse-windows-active-directory-privilege-escalation-51d8519a13d7)

NOTE: might need to gpupdate /force
